phrasal_verbs.meaning: be persuaded to change your mind (about)
stop making an effort to achieve sth difficult
draw a line through sth written
stay at the same level as
if something dawns on you, you realise it for the first time
do something or deal with something very easily
explain, describe or arrange sth in a clear and detailed way
leave school, etc before you have finished a course

discussion: have a discussion (with sb) about/on sth/doing

A.title: Topic vocabulary in contrast
A.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

questions.sentence: The community centre's after-school club currently has around thirty ...... attending each week.
Two senior members of the youth group were selected as ...... to help guide the newer volunteers.
The adult education centre welcomed over three hundred new ...... at the start of the autumn term.
By the end of the fundraising drive, the neighbourhood association had ...... its target of ten thousand pounds.
Residents are encouraged to ...... the highest standard of participation in local events.
A retired craftsman from our street ...... many local young people how to repair and restore old furniture.
Local volunteers are eager to ...... new skills at the free community workshops held every Saturday morning.
Children attending the ...... school on Maple Avenue took part in the neighbourhood mural project.
The ...... school near the park organised an open careers fair that all local residents were welcome to attend.
The new community sports hall is located just next to the ...... school on the north side of town.
The survey ...... were published last week and showed strong support for more green spaces in the area.
Every volunteer who completed the first aid training course received a ...... at the end of the session.
The new community liaison officer holds a ...... in social sciences from a leading university.

B.title: Circle the correct word
B.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

questions.sentence: After months of careful preparation, the young mechanic was thrilled to find out she had **passed / took** her practical assessment.
It is often difficult to **count / measure** the true impact that a community project has on local residents.
Everyone was impressed that the guest speaker at the neighbourhood event could **speak / talk** four languages.
The job advertisement for the new community coordinator listed several **qualifications / qualities** that applicants were required to hold.
I spotted my old neighbour at the street fair but barely **know / recognise** him after so many years away.
Local residents were given the **effort / task** of decorating the community hall for the annual street party.
Sustainable living has become a very popular **subject / lesson** at the local community workshops.
If you want to qualify as a community support worker, you will need to **read / study** for at least three years.
Before joining the volunteer fire brigade, all new recruits must pass a standard fitness **exam / test**.
At the reunion event, she was delighted to catch up with some of her old **colleagues / classmates** from primary school.

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: The committee chair asked everyone to get ...... with reading the new proposals while she made some tea.
Given her years of experience organising local events, everyone expected her to sail ...... the interview with ease.
Nobody could quite work out what the local councillor was getting ...... in his rather vague speech.
Residents were sceptical about the new recycling scheme at first, but eventually came ...... to supporting it.
She noticed she had written the wrong address on the form and quickly crossed it ...... before handing it in.
He missed several community meetings and found it increasingly difficult to keep ...... with all the decisions being made.
The new parking restrictions were introduced without much publicity, and many drivers didn't catch ...... for weeks.

D.title: Phrasal verbs (transformation)
D.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
The ideas behind your proposal need to be presented more clearly.
**set** You need to ...... in your proposal more clearly.

You should consider the college's offer carefully rather than rushing into a decision.
**over** Why not take a few days to ...... before making a decision?

The community volunteers were determined to keep going despite strong opposition from the council.
**in** The community volunteers refused to ...... despite strong opposition from the council.

When she first started volunteering at the centre, Paula really struggled to manage all the work.
**deal** Paula really struggled to ...... when she first started volunteering at the centre.

I suddenly realised that I had left the event tickets at home.
**dawned** It ...... that I had left the event tickets at home.

Feeling overwhelmed by the workload, Ed decided to leave university after just one term.
**out** Ed ...... after just one term because he felt overwhelmed by the workload.
The neighbourhood festival managed to ...... a great deal of attention from local businesses and sponsors.
All volunteers are expected to ...... their fair share of tasks before the community event begins.
Would it be alright if I ...... a suggestion about the layout of the new community garden?
Most residents couldn't ...... the point of closing the high street car park every weekend.
The volunteers gathered in the car park during the lunch ...... to share refreshments and chat.
Could you pass that map ...... to me, please? I'd like to check the route.
The residents' association announced that they are ...... their next meeting in the local café.
Last night, the neighbours had a long discussion ...... the proposed road closure near the park.
...... my opinion, the new bus route will greatly benefit elderly residents in the area.
All new volunteers are asked to ...... a short assessment before joining the community response team.
I'm still in ...... minds about whether to apply for the position of community representative.
There is always a great deal ...... when you get involved with local community projects.
The chairperson asked everyone to speak clearly so that the recording would ...... sense.
When asked about the missing event funds, the treasurer had ...... idea where they had gone.

F.title: Word patterns (error correction)
F.instructions: Each of the words in bold is incorrect. Rewrite them correctly.

questions.sentence: Local residents have long admired the neighbourhood warden **from** dealing with problems so calmly.
The new community hall is quite similar **with** the one that was demolished last spring.
The mayor congratulated the volunteers **for** organising such a well-attended charity event.
Our trainer advised us to revise **on** the health and safety regulations before the final assessment.
The new coordinator is perfectly capable **for** managing the entire neighbourhood project on her own.
The meeting room is suitable **to** groups of up to twelve people and can be booked in advance.
The community couldn't afford the original design, so they had to settle **with** a smaller playground.
After months of campaigning, the residents finally succeeded **with** getting the dangerous road repaired.

G.title: Cloze (Word patterns)
G.instructions: Water has damaged part of this text from a diary. Read it and decide what you think each of the original words was. Write the words in the blank spaces.

questions.sentence: I was able ...... introduce myself to a few of the regular volunteers, though I hope to meet more next week.
There was one man I found quite irritating, who boasted ...... his previous experience and succeeded ...... putting everyone off.
The group leader looked at my background and said she failed ...... see how I would fit into their current programme.
I know I'm just as capable ...... contributing as anyone else in the group.
I'm determined to prepare hard ...... the next community event and show everyone what I can do.

H.title: Word formation (sentences)
H.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

questions.sentence: The local council offered a generous ...... (SCHOLAR) to the most promising young musician in the area.
The neighbourhood watch coordinator asked all residents to pay ...... (ATTEND) to the new safety guidelines.
After weeks of debate, the planning committee finally found a ...... (SOLVE) to the ongoing parking problem.
The young volunteer had to balance her community work carefully with her ...... (STUDY) at the local college.
The adult education centre is currently looking for experienced ...... (TEACH) to run evening classes for local residents.
The community centre had to address growing concerns about the ...... (BEHAVE) of some younger visitors.
She spent the entire weekend doing ...... (REVISE) for her professional development qualification.
All applicants are required to bring photocopies of their relevant ...... (CERTIFY) to the selection interview.

I.title: Word formation (text)
I.instructions: Complete the text by changing the form of the word in capitals.

questions.sentence: It seems (1) ...... (THINK) today not to invest in adult learning programmes within local communities. A good (2) ...... (EDUCATE) should be available to people of all ages.
There is such an emphasis on (3) ...... (ACADEMY) qualifications these days that the serious problem of adult (4) ...... (LITERATE) is sometimes overlooked.
Discovering that you cannot read properly can be (5) ...... (INTENSE) distressing and can make a person feel like a complete (6) ...... (FAIL).
People in this situation are (7) ...... (UNDERSTAND) reluctant to seek help or draw attention to themselves in public.
The challenge can sometimes appear (8) ...... (SOLVE), especially without the right kind of support.
However, with the right mentor, consistent effort, and a (9) ...... (REASON) amount of patience, real progress is achievable.
Getting involved in a community learning programme can lead to a noticeably (10) ...... (IMPROVE) quality of life.
